rules.title: Only registered workflow node types may appear
rules.description: Generated or repaired graphs must stay within the existing workflow node catalog and may not invent new node types.
antiPatterns: Invent a node type because its name sounds suitable for the requested business step.
examples: Use an existing condition, tool, LLM, or end node and express the business behavior through supported configuration.
rules.repairHint: Replace unsupported nodes with the closest existing platform node type and rewire the flow around the supported catalog only.

rules.title: Tool calls must map to approved tools
rules.description: Tool nodes must reference approved platform tools rather than unsupported business-side placeholders.
antiPatterns: Configure a plausible CRM, logistics, notification, or ticket tool name that is absent from the runtime registry.
examples: Select an exact registered tool name; when no suitable tool exists, clarify the limitation or route to a human.
rules.repairHint: Swap placeholder tool names for approved platform tools and move unsupported business logic into prompts or existing routing nodes.

rules.title: Prompt templates and output contracts must agree
rules.description: Structured-output prompts, declared schemas, and downstream variable usage should not contradict one another.
antiPatterns: Ask the model for one set of fields while downstream conditions reference different or undeclared fields.
examples: Declare every field used by downstream nodes and use the same names and value vocabulary in the prompt.
rules.repairHint: Align the prompt, declared output schema, and downstream variable references so every consumed field is actually produced.

rules.title: Final answers must remain customer-readable
rules.description: Terminal customer-facing responses should avoid raw internal fields, debugging fragments, and incomplete placeholders.
antiPatterns: Return raw JSON, unresolved template variables, internal routing labels, or stack traces to the customer.
examples: Convert internal results into a concise natural-language answer that states the outcome and next step.
rules.repairHint: Rewrite the terminal response into customer-facing language and remove internal variables, debugging text, or unfinished placeholders.

rules.title: Do not fabricate unsupported actions or external capabilities
rules.description: The workflow must not promise business outcomes or represent an LLM-only step as external search, browsing, lookup, or another unavailable capability.
antiPatterns: State that a refund, compensation, shipment, or account action succeeded when no approved tool or policy result supports it.
Tell an LLM to simulate Web Search, multi-source retrieval, or browsing when no executable search tool exists.
examples: Explain what can be verified now and request required information or escalate before committing to a business outcome.
Use the registered tavily_search node for external web research, then make downstream LLM nodes consume its results.
If Tavily is not configured, stop with a clear configuration requirement instead of simulating search.
rules.repairHint: Replace simulated web search with the registered tavily_search node and make downstream nodes consume its results. Never put an API key in workflow config; ask the user to configure Tavily in Settings when the integration is unavailable.

rules.title: Generated paths must fit the automated runtime budget
rules.description: A single execution path may contain at most two serial LLM calls so mandatory cases can finish within the 90-second evaluation budget.
antiPatterns: Chain separate LLM nodes for parsing, simulated search, synthesis, and report formatting on the same path.
examples: Combine adjacent reasoning and formatting stages into one bounded LLM node, or replace external work with an approved tool followed by one answer node.
rules.repairHint: Reduce each path to at most two LLM calls by merging adjacent prompts and replacing simulated external work with tavily_search.

rules.title: HTTP requests need a real user-approved endpoint
rules.description: HTTP request nodes may not ship with placeholder domains or model-invented endpoints.
antiPatterns: Generate example.com, example.test, localhost, or a plausible unconfirmed API URL.
examples: Use the exact URL confirmed in the locked specification, or keep the blueprint blocked for user configuration.
rules.repairHint: Remove the placeholder endpoint and request the exact API URL without discarding the editable blueprint.

rules.title: HTTP secrets must use managed credentials
rules.description: Workflow JSON must never contain authorization tokens, API keys, passwords, cookies, or embedded URL credentials.
antiPatterns: Put Authorization, X-API-Key, token, password, or cookie values in headers, params, URL, or body config.
examples: Reference an existing owner-scoped credentialId and keep secret material in the credential center.
rules.repairHint: Delete inline secret material and bind a matching managed credential; if none exists, mark credential configuration as blocking.

rules.title: HTTP failure outcomes need an explicit consumer
rules.description: HTTP 4xx and 5xx responses should be checked through statusCode or succeeded before the workflow claims success.
antiPatterns: Send an HTTP response directly to a success answer without checking succeeded or statusCode.
examples: Route on nodes.request.succeeded or nodes.request.statusCode and provide a useful failure branch.
rules.repairHint: Add a downstream condition that consumes succeeded or statusCode and handles the unsuccessful branch.

rules.title: HTTP consumers must use the fixed response contract
rules.description: Downstream templates may only consume statusCode, headers, body, json, durationMs, or succeeded from HTTP nodes.
antiPatterns: Reference invented fields such as response, data, result, code, or output directly on an HTTP node.
examples: Use nodes.request.json for parsed JSON or nodes.request.body for text.
rules.repairHint: Replace invented HTTP output paths with a field from the fixed HTTP response contract.

rules.title: Report exports require a reachable upstream string
rules.description: Report content must be an exact string output from a reachable upstream node, normally the final report-writing LLM answer.
antiPatterns: Export input.message directly, reference a downstream node, or embed the report body in workflow JSON.
examples: Set content to nodes.llm_report.answer after the report-writing node is connected upstream.
rules.repairHint: Connect a report-writing node before report_export and select its string output through the variable graph.

rules.title: Report file names must be safe
rules.description: Report file names may not contain path traversal, control characters, path separators, or executable extensions.
antiPatterns: Use ../report.exe, a shell-script extension, or a path instead of a base file name.
examples: Use a plain business name such as quarterly-research-report; the exporter appends the selected extension.
rules.repairHint: Replace the value with a plain base file name and let report_export append the selected safe extension.

rules.title: Report export formats and limits must be valid
rules.description: A report export must select at least one supported format and stay inside the configured retention and size limits.
antiPatterns: Use an unsupported format, select no format, retain artifacts beyond 365 days, or try to embed oversized file data.
examples: Select one or more of pdf, docx, html, markdown, or txt and keep retentionDays between 1 and 365.
rules.repairHint: Choose supported formats, remove embedded file data, and restore the configured retention and size limits.

rules.title: Report rendering must stay inside the safe document profile
rules.description: Report prompts may not request scripts, event handlers, remote images, data URLs, or custom CSS. HTML is sanitized and external resources are disabled.
antiPatterns: Ask an LLM to emit script tags, remote image tags, JavaScript URLs, or arbitrary CSS for export.
examples: Ask for Markdown headings, paragraphs, lists, tables, and code blocks; select a built-in theme.
rules.repairHint: Rewrite the report prompt to produce Markdown content and use business, minimal, or academic theme settings.

rules.title: Variable aggregator candidates must be valid upstream outputs
rules.description: Every aggregator candidate must reference an existing reachable upstream node and match its declared output type.
antiPatterns: Aggregate a state field, a downstream node, an incomplete template, or a candidate of the wrong type.
examples: Select typed upstream variables through the visual picker and preserve candidate order.
rules.repairHint: Replace invalid candidates with typed outputs from reachable upstream nodes and keep group keys unique.

knowledgeEntries: All governance packs are immutable, versioned inputs to later validation, repair, and runtime checks.
The core pack always applies even when no domain-specific pack is selected.
Workflow governance cannot expand the node catalog; it constrains usage of existing nodes and tools only.
Detector names in this pack are data-only labels for later executable validators.

testCases.prompt: Build a customer workflow that uses existing start, llm, tool, condition, and end nodes only.
testCases.expectedBehavior: The workflow stays inside the registered node catalog and avoids model-invented node types.

testCases.prompt: Tell the customer we guarantee a full refund even if no order or policy lookup succeeds.
testCases.expectedBehavior: The workflow refuses unsupported guarantees and routes through clarification or verified data instead.
